content: Date: 2026-03-24. Idea: a tiny CLI habit tracker that guilt-trips me gently. I want something I can call from terminal in <5s, stores data locally, and nudges me with one-line guilt messages that feel personal but not abusive. Name: working title "cli-guilt" (binary: guilt). Goals: MVP v0.1 by 2026-04-07 (two weeks), approx 12–16 hours of my time total. Core constraints: single static binary, no network telemetry, config & DB in ~/.config/cli-guilt/, MIT license, publish on GitHub sam/cli-guilt and a Homebrew tap. Minimal commands: add, do, status, history, set-tone. Keep UX minimal: default show for today when invoked with no args.
What's the single core action that must work before v0.1 release?

Single core action: fast logging — "guilt do <habit>" should be 1-liner, update streaks, and print an immediate guilt message. Examples: 
- guilt do read
- guilt do meditate --when 2026-03-24T08:30
- guilt add "no-sugar" --days weekdays
Data model (JSON file at ~/.config/cli-guilt/db.json): each habit: {"id":"read","name":"Read 30m","schedule":"daily","streak":5,"last":"2026-03-23","history":["2026-03-20","2026-03-21",...]}.
Escalation rules (MVP): if missed 1 day → gentle hint; 2–3 days → firmer; >=4 days → sharp. Guilt messages should include name and last streak and 30-day adherence % (e.g., 80%). Prefer Go for a single static binary (clap-like flags via cobra/urfave) because I want fast cross-compiles and simple Homebrew release. Implementation estimate: ~8–12h to get add/do/status + tests, 4h packaging + formula. Privacy: local only, no analytics.
Do you want the guilt messages personalized (use your name and past stats) or generic?

Yes personalized: use my name "Sam" and a stat line. Templates with placeholders like {name},{habit},{streak},{missed},{pct30}. Example messages:
- gentle / missed=1: "Sam — missed yesterday. One small step now? (streak 5 → 4, 30d: 80%)"
- stern / missed=2: "Sam — two days off. Start now or it becomes three. (streak 3, 30d: 67%)"
- shame / missed>=4: "Sam — 4+ days. You know better. Pick one minute and begin. (30d: 40%)"
Tone default: gentle. Allow set-tone gentle|stern|shame per-user in config. Keep all personalization local; no templates fetched.
Also: default pre-configured habits for alpha: 1) read (daily), 2) meditate (daily 10m), 3) no-sugar (weekdays). CLI extra flags: --quiet to suppress guilt, --simulate to test messages. Tests: unit for schedule checks and message rendering. Ship v0.1.0 on 2026-04-07 to sam/cli-guilt with README examples and sample config.
Homebrew tap and single binary with Go sounds good — do you want to publish pre-installed default habits in the repo or require 'guilt add' for first-run?

Pre-install defaults in a sample config committed to repo (config/default.json) so first-run does: copy defaults to ~/.config/cli-guilt/config.json if missing. README must show exact commands and examples: 
- guilt do read  # logs today and prints guilt message
- guilt status   # table: habit | streak | last | 30d%
- guilt history read --days 30  # CSV or pretty table
- guilt set-tone stern
UI details: ANSI colors — gentle = dim green/yellow, stern = bright yellow, shame = bold bright red. One-line output max 120 chars. Example CLI output for "guilt do read":
[green]✓[/green] Read — logged 2026-03-24. Sam — missed yesterday. One small step now? (streak 6, 30d: 83%)
Config: ~/.config/cli-guilt/config.json (contains name="Sam", tone="gentle", timezone). Repo: sam/cli-guilt, license MIT, tag v0.1.0 on 2026-04-07.
Which exact ANSI color choices and the 1-line wording for a 1-day miss in 'gentle' do you want (give the exact text and which color to apply)?

Gentle 1-day miss exact: color: dim yellow for the message text, green checkmark. Wording (exact): "✓ Read — logged 2026-03-24. Sam — missed yesterday. One small step now? (streak 6, 30d: 83%)"  
- Prefix: green "✓" (U+2713) then habit name in default, then "— logged <date>." then message in dim yellow, then stat paren in dim gray. 
Stern 2-day miss exact: "! Read — two days off. Start now or it becomes three. (streak 4, 30d: 67%)" color: bright yellow message, exclamation mark in yellow. 
Shame >=4: "✗ Read — 4 days off. You know better. Pick one minute and begin. (30d: 40%)" color: bold bright red with red X (U+2717). 
Keep max 120 chars. These exact strings go into template store in config/templates.json so I can tweak later. Tests should assert output contains these exact strings and proper color escapes.

Plan summary: Go single binary, JSON store at ~/.config/cli-guilt/, commands add/do/status/history/set-tone, three default habits, personalized templates and ANSI colors as specified, Homebrew release, target v0.1.0 on 2026-04-07. Ready for me to init the git repo and generate the initial README + default config now?
